ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 (ScarCruft) CHM / LNK initial access — hh.exe or mshta.exe spawning PowerShell stager
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical APT37 / ScarCruft / Reaper delivery chain: a weaponised Compiled HTML Help (.chm) or LNK/HWP attachment is opened, causing hh.exe (CHM viewer) or mshta.exe to execute embedded script that spawns PowerShell to fetch a RokRat / Chinotto / M2RAT loader. APT37 has used this exact CHM→mshta/hh→PowerShell chain in multiple 2022-2024 campaigns against South Korean defectors, journalists, and human-rights NGOs (RedEyes/ScarCruft reporting from AhnLab and Recorded Future).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("hh.exe","mshta.exe") OR (Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe" AND Processes.parent_process IN ("*.chm*","*.lnk*","*.hwp*"))) Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(process,"(?i)(IEX|Invoke-Expression|DownloadString|DownloadFile|FromBase64String|-EncodedCommand|-enc |WebClient|hxxp|Net\.WebRequest|Start-BitsTransfer|certutil|bitsadmin|pcloud|yandex|dropbox)") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft — CHM or mshta delivery chain spawning a PowerShell/script stager
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe"]);
let _stager_terms     = dynamic(["IEX","Invoke-Expression","DownloadString","DownloadFile","FromBase64String","-EncodedCommand","-enc ","WebClient","Net.WebRequest","Start-BitsTransfer","certutil","bitsadmin","pcloud","yandex","dropbox","pcloud.com","box.com"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                         // exclude machine accounts
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("hh.exe","mshta.exe")  // CHM viewer or HTA host
   or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "explorer.exe"
       and InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (".chm",".lnk",".hwp",".doc",".docx"))
| where FileName in~ (_suspicious_children)
| extend _has_stager = ProcessCommandLine has_any (_stager_terms)
      or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
| where _has_stager
   or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("hh.exe","mshta.exe")  // hh.exe spawning ANY script host is anomalous
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          IsRemote    = InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft — CHM or mshta delivery chain (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe"]);
let _stager_terms     = dynamic(["IEX","Invoke-Expression","DownloadString","DownloadFile","FromBase64String","-EncodedCommand","-enc ","WebClient","Net.WebRequest","Start-BitsTransfer","certutil","bitsadmin","pcloud","yandex","dropbox"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User" and Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in ("hh.exe","mshta.exe")
   or (ParentLeaf == "explorer.exe" and CommandLine has_any (".chm",".lnk",".hwp"))
| where ChildLeaf in (_suspicious_children)
| extend _has_stager = CommandLine has_any (_stager_terms)
      or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
| where _has_stager or ParentLeaf in ("hh.exe","mshta.exe")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, ParentLeaf,
          NewProcessName, ChildLeaf,
          CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT37 ScarCruft CHM or HTA Loader Spawning Script Stager
id: 9f1d3a4e-37ab-4c3f-8c0e-2d3f12a3b7de
status: experimental
description: Detects APT37 (ScarCruft / Reaper / Group123) initial access pattern where a Compiled HTML Help file or HTA causes hh.exe or mshta.exe to spawn a PowerShell / script-host stager containing download or base64 indicators consistent with RokRat / Chinotto / M2RAT loaders.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0067/
  - https://asec.ahnlab.com/en/55229/
  - https://www.recordedfuture.com/scarcruft-malware-analysis
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1218.001
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.g0067
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_chm_hta:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\hh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
  parent_explorer_lure:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\explorer.exe'
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - '.chm'
      - '.lnk'
      - '.hwp'
  child_script_host:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
  stager_indicators:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'IEX'
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'DownloadFile'
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - '-enc '
      - 'WebClient'
      - 'Start-BitsTransfer'
      - 'pcloud'
      - 'yandex'
      - 'dropbox'
  filter_machine_accounts:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: child_script_host and (parent_chm_hta or parent_explorer_lure) and (parent_chm_hta or stager_indicators) and not filter_machine_accounts
fields:
  - ComputerName
  - User
  - ParentImage
  - ParentCommandLine
  - Image
  - CommandLine
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IT documentation packaged as CHM that opens an external URL handler
  - Software installers that ship CHM help files invoking installers
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT37 has shifted heavily to CHM-based lures since ~2022 (documented by AhnLab ASEC, Recorded Future, S2W Talon, and Genians) — hh.exe spawning PowerShell or mshta is essentially never legitimate end-user behaviour, while explorer.exe→CHM/LNK/HWP→script host captures the LNK and HWP variants of the same kit. Pairing the parent (hh.exe/mshta.exe/CHM-loaded explorer) with a script-host child plus st

ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 RokRat / Chinotto C2 — non-browser process beaconing to pCloud / Yandex / Dropbox API endpoints
ucs.description: Hunts APT37's hallmark C2 channel: RokRat, Chinotto, Dolphin, and M2RAT all abuse legitimate cloud-storage APIs (api.pcloud.com, cloud-api.yandex.net, content.dropboxapi.com, box.com) for command-and-control and exfiltration so traffic blends with normal HTTPS. We surface non-browser, non-Office processes contacting these specific API hostnames — extremely characteristic of ScarCruft tooling per Cisco Talos, ESET, and Volexity reporting.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.dest) as remote_hosts values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as remote_ips values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as remote_ports min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest IN ("*api.pcloud.com","*eapi.pcloud.com","*cloud-api.yandex.net","*webdav.yandex.*","*content.dropboxapi.com","*api.dropboxapi.com","*api.box.com","*upload.box.com")) NOT All_Traffic.app IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","safari.exe","OneDrive.exe","Dropbox.exe","YandexDisk.exe","pCloud.exe","Box.exe","WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.process_name All_Traffic.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | where count > 5 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft RokRat / Chinotto / Dolphin C2 — non-browser process to cloud-storage APIs
let _apt37_c2_hosts = dynamic([
    "api.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","binapi.pcloud.com",
    "cloud-api.yandex.net","webdav.yandex.com","webdav.yandex.ru",
    "content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com",
    "api.box.com","upload.box.com"]);
let _legit_clients = dynamic([
    "chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe",
    "OneDrive.exe","Dropbox.exe","DropboxUpdate.exe","YandexDisk.exe",
    "pCloud.exe","pCloudDrive.exe","Box.exe","Box Drive.exe","BoxSync.exe",
    "Teams.exe","slack.exe","OUTLOOK.EXE"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (_apt37_c2_hosts)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_legit_clients)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
// Exclude signed Microsoft / vendor binaries that legitimately call these APIs as part of integrations
| where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName !has_any ("Dropbox","Yandex","pCloud","Box, Inc","Microsoft Corporation")
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(Timestamp, 1m)),
            UrlSamples = make_set(RemoteUrl, 10),
            RemoteIPs  = make_set(RemoteIP, 10)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
               InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
               InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where ConnCount >= 3              // any sustained beaconing — RokRat polls every ~60s
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT37 RokRat / Chinotto C2 — Sentinel ASIM network sessions with web-session fallback
let _apt37_c2_hosts = dynamic([
    "api.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","binapi.pcloud.com",
    "cloud-api.yandex.net","webdav.yandex.com","webdav.yandex.ru",
    "content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com",
    "api.box.com","upload.box.com"]);
let _legit_clients = dynamic([
    "chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe",
    "OneDrive.exe","Dropbox.exe","YandexDisk.exe","pCloud.exe","Box.exe","BoxSync.exe",
    "Teams.exe","slack.exe","OUTLOOK.EXE"]);
let _net = ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where isnotempty(DstHostname)
    | where DstHostname has_any (_apt37_c2_hosts)
    | where ActingProcessName !in~ (_legit_clients)
    | where SrcUsername !endswith "$"
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, SrcUsername, ActingProcessName,
              ActingProcessCommandLine, DstHostname, DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber, Url;
let _web = ImWebSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where HttpHostName has_any (_apt37_c2_hosts)
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, SrcUsername,
              ActingProcessName = "", ActingProcessCommandLine = "",
              DstHostname = HttpHostName, DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber, Url;
union isfuzzy=true _net, _web
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)),
            HostsContacted = make_set(DstHostname, 10),
            Urls = make_set(Url, 10)
            by DvcHostname, SrcUsername, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine
| where ConnCount >= 3
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: APT37's Dolphin (ESET 2022), Chinotto (Volexity / Kaspersky), RokRat (Talos), and M2RAT (AhnLab) families have all been documented using pCloud, Yandex Cloud, and Dropbox API endpoints for C2 — this is a near-signature behaviour that distinguishes ScarCruft from other DPRK actors (Lazarus prefers compromised infrastructure; Kimsuky prefers attacker-controlled VPS). Filtering to non-browser, non-ve
